logging(3) Library Functions Manual logging(3)

NAME

logging - Logging System


- 📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

SYNOPSIS

Files


file colorize.c
Log message colorization for terminal output. file format.c
Log format parser implementation. file json.c
📝 JSON structured logging output using yyjson file log.c
📝 Multi-level logging with terminal color support, file rotation, and async output file mmap.c
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging implementation. file named.c
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file colorize.h
Log message colorization for terminal output. file json.h
📝 JSON structured logging output file log.h
📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control file mmap.h
Lock-free memory-mapped text logging with crash safety. file named.h
Format named objects in log messages (replaces hex addresses with type/name descriptions). file types.h
Log level types and constants.

Data Structures


struct log_buffer_entry_t
A single buffered log entry.

#define DEFAULT_LOG_LEVEL LOG_DEBUG
Default log level for debug builds (DEBUG and above). #define MAX_LOG_SIZE (3 * 1024 * 1024)
Maximum log file size in bytes (3MB) before rotation. #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_SIZE (64 * 1024)
Maximum size of terminal output buffer (64KB). #define MAX_TERMINAL_BUFFER_ENTRIES 256
Maximum number of buffered log entries. #define LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 4096
Maximum size of a single log message (including formatting). #define LOG_MMAP_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE 1024
Maximum size of a log message in mmap mode. #define LOG_HEADER_BUFFER_SIZE 512
Maximum size of a log header (timestamp, level, file:line:func). #define LOG_TIMESTAMP_BUFFER_SIZE 32
Maximum size of a timestamp string. #define log_plain(...)
Plain logging - writes to both log file and stderr without timestamps or log levels. #define log_plain_stderr(...)
Plain logging to stderr with newline. #define log_plain_stdout(...)
Plain logging to stdout with newline. #define log_plain_stderr_nonewline(...)
Plain logging to stderr without newline - for interactive prompts. #define log_file(...)
File-only logging - writes to log file only, no stderr output. #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)
Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). #define log_nth(log_level, n, fmt, ...)
Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). #define log_once(log_level, fmt, ...)
Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). #define LOG_CLIENT_IMPL(client, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends DEBUG log message to client. #define log_info_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends INFO log message to client. #define log_warn_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends WARN log message to client. #define log_error_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends ERROR log message to client. #define log_fatal_client(client, fmt, ...)
Server sends FATAL log message to client. #define LOG_SERVER_IMPL(sockfd, crypto_ctx, level, fmt, ...)
#define log_debug_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends DEBUG log message to server. #define log_info_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends INFO log message to server. #define log_warn_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends WARN log message to server. #define log_error_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends ERROR log message to server. #define log_fatal_server(sockfd, crypto_ctx, fmt, ...)
Client sends FATAL log message to server.

Logging levels enumeration. enum remote_log_direction { REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN = 0, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_SERVER_TO_CLIENT = 1, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER = 2 }

Logging Macros

Note

Compile-time log level stripping: In release builds, log_dev() and log_debug() are compiled out completely (no runtime overhead). Override with LOG_COMPILE_LEVEL.


#define log_dev(...)
Log a DEV message (most verbose, development only). #define log_debug(...)
Log a DEBUG message. #define log_info(...)
Log an INFO message. #define log_warn(...)
Log a WARN message. #define log_error(...)
Log an ERROR message. #define log_fatal(...)
Log a FATAL message.

Detailed Description

📝 Logging API with multiple log levels and terminal output control

This header provides a comprehensive logging system with:

  • Multiple log levels (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL)
  • File and terminal output with automatic color coding
  • Terminal capability detection (16-color, 256-color, truecolor)
  • Rate-limited logging macros for high-frequency logging
  • Thread-safe logging operations
  • Automatic log file rotation when size limit is reached

Note

In debug builds, log macros include file/line/function information. In release builds, this information is omitted.

This module formats named objects in log messages by scanning for hex addresses and replacing them with friendly descriptions from the named object registry when available.

For example:

  • Input: "Locked 0x7f1234567890 successfully"
  • Output: "Locked mutex: recv_mutex.2 (0x7f1234567890) successfully"

When an address is not registered, it's left unchanged.

  if ((client)->crypto_initialized) {                                                                                \

#define
  log_every( log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \


  uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \


  uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \


  if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \


    if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \


      log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \


    }                                                                                                                \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Rate-limited logging macro (thread-safe). Logs at most once per
    specified time interval. Useful for threads that have an FPS and functions
    they call to prevent spammy logs.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


interval_us Minimum microseconds between log messages


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Each call site maintains its own static atomic timer, so
  different call sites can log independently. Thread-safe via atomic
  compare-exchange.



Uses platform_get_monotonic_time_us() for cross-platform
    time.




Definition at line 671 of file log/log.h.


671 #define log_every(log_level, interval_us, fmt, ...)                                                                    \
672   do {                                                                                                                 \
673     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_every_last_time = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                         \
674     uint64_t _log_every_now = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();                                                        \
675     uint64_t _log_every_last = atomic_load_u64(&_log_every_last_time);                      \
676     if (_log_every_now - _log_every_last >= (uint64_t)(interval_us)) {                                                 \
677       if (atomic_cas_u64(&_log_every_last_time, &_log_every_last, _log_every_now)) {                                   \
678         log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                    \
679       }                                                                                                                \
680     }                                                                                                                  \
681   } while (0)

#define
  log_nth( log_level, n, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \


  uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \


  uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \


  atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \


  if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log every nth call to this code location (thread-safe). Logs a
    message every nth time the code is executed. Useful for logging periodic
    events in tight loops without spamming the log. For example, log_nth(INFO,
    1000, "Processed items") logs every 1000 calls.




Each call site maintains its own static counter, so different call
    sites can log independently at different frequencies.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


n Log every nth call (1 = every call, 2 = every 2nd call, etc.)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic fetch_add.

740   do {                                                                                                                 \
741     static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_nth_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                             \
742     uint64_t _log_nth_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_nth_counter);                           \
743     uint64_t _log_nth_new = _log_nth_count + 1;                                                                        \
744     atomic_store_u64(&_log_nth_counter, _log_nth_new);                                      \
745     if (_log_nth_new % (uint64_t)(n) == 0) {                                                                           \
746       log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \
747     }                                                                                                                  \
748   } while (0)










#define
  log_once( log_level, fmt, ...)


#include
    <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>Value:


do {                                                                                                                 \


  static LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64 _log_once_counter = LOG_ATOMIC_UINT64_INIT(0);                                            \


  uint64_t _log_once_count = atomic_load_u64(&_log_once_counter);                         \


  if (_log_once_count == 0) {                                                                                        \


    atomic_store_u64(&_log_once_counter, 1);                                              \


    log_msg(LOG_##log_level, __FILE__, __LINE__, __func__, fmt, ##__VA_ARGS__);                                      \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)






Log exactly once per call site (thread-safe). Logs a message
    exactly once, no matter how many times the code is executed. Each call site
    maintains its own static counter, so different call sites can log
    independently.




Useful for one-time initialization messages, warnings, or debug
    output that should only appear once per session.




Parameters


log_level Log level (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN,
  ERROR, FATAL)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Note


Thread-safe via atomic operations.

enum log_color_t


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Color enum for logging - indexes into color arrays. These values
    directly index into level_colors arrays. Order matches DEV, DEBUG, WARN,
    INFO, ERROR, FATAL, GREY, RESET.


Enumerator
  





  
LOG_COLOR_DEV
    

  
Blue - DEV messages

  
LOG_COLOR_DEBUG
    

  
Cyan - DEBUG messages

  
LOG_COLOR_INFO
    

  
Green - INFO messages

  
LOG_COLOR_WARN
    

  
Yellow - WARN messages

  
LOG_COLOR_ERROR
    

  
Red - ERROR messages

  
LOG_COLOR_FATAL
    

  
Magenta - FATAL messages

  
LOG_COLOR_GREY
    

  
Grey - for neutral messages or labels

  
LOG_COLOR_RESET
    

  
Reset to default

130   LOG_COLOR_FATAL = 5,

Function

const
  char * colorize_named_string (const char * name_str)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Colorize a "type/name" string for display.


Parameters


name_str String in "type/name" or
  "type/name.id" format


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes:
  type(yellow)/name(blue)


Used by memory report and other displays to colorize named
    objects. Applies colors:


    
  
  • Type: yellow (LOG_COLOR_WARN)
    • 
  
  • Name: blue (LOG_COLOR_DEV)
    • 
  
  • Separator: uncolored
    • 



Example: "thread/splash_anim.0" displays with type in
    yellow, name in blue




Note


Returns pointer to rotating static buffer (4 buffers)



Safe to use in printf-style calls: "%s"




Colorize a "type/name" string for display.




Parameters


name_str String in "type/name
  (0xaddress)" format (e.g., "thread/splash_anim.0
  (0x7f123456)")


Returns


Colorized string with ANSI codes: type(yellow)/name(blue)
  (id(grey))


Used by memory report to colorize named object display. Returns a
    pointer to a static rotating buffer (4 buffers, valid until next call).




Example: "thread/splash_anim.0 (0x7f123456)" displays
    with:


    
  
  • "thread" in yellow
    • 
  
  • "splash_anim.0" in blue
    • 
  
  • "0x7f123456" in grey
    • 



Definition at line 797 of file log/named.c.


797                                                         {
798 #define COLORIZE_BUFFERS 4
799 #define COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE 512
800   static char buffers[COLORIZE_BUFFERS][COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE];
801   static int buffer_idx = 0;
802 
803   if (!name_str || name_str[0] == '\0') {
804     return name_str;
805   }
806 
807   char *current_buf = buffers[buffer_idx];
808   buffer_idx = (buffer_idx + 1) % COLORIZE_BUFFERS;
809 
810   /* Find the slash separator (type/name) */
811   const char *slash = strchr(name_str, '/');
812   if (!slash) {
813     /* No slash found - just return the string as-is */
814     return name_str;
815   }
816 
817   /* Find opening paren for address */
818   const char *paren = strchr(name_str, '(');
819 
820   /* Extract type (before slash) */
821   size_t type_len = slash - name_str;
822   char type_buf[256];
823   strncpy(type_buf, name_str, type_len < sizeof(type_buf) - 1 ? type_len : sizeof(type_buf) - 1);
824   type_buf[type_len] = '\0';
825 
826   /* Extract name (between slash and paren, or end of string) */
827   const char *name_start = slash + 1;
828   size_t name_len;
829   if (paren && paren > slash) {
830     /* Skip spaces before paren */
831     const char *name_end = paren - 1;
832     while (name_end > name_start && *name_end == ' ') {
833       name_end--;
834     }
835     name_len = name_end - name_start + 1;
836   } else {
837     name_len = strlen(name_start);
838   }
839 
840   char name_buf[256];
841   strncpy(name_buf, name_start, name_len < sizeof(name_buf) - 1 ? name_len : sizeof(name_buf) - 1);
842   name_buf[name_len] = '\0';
843 
844   /* Apply colors */
845   const char *type_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, type_buf);
846   const char *name_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEV, name_buf);
847 
848   /* Format: type(yellow)/name(blue) and add address part if present */
849   if (paren) {
850     /* Extract address (0x...) from parentheses */
851     const char *addr_start = paren + 1;
852     const char *addr_end = strchr(addr_start, ')');
853     if (addr_end) {
854       size_t addr_len = addr_end - addr_start;
855       char addr_buf[128];
856       strncpy(addr_buf, addr_start, addr_len < sizeof(addr_buf) - 1 ? addr_len : sizeof(addr_buf) - 1);
857       addr_buf[addr_len] = '\0';
858 
859       const char *addr_colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, addr_buf);
860       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s (%s)", type_colored, name_colored, addr_colored);
861     } else {
862       safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s %s", type_colored, name_colored, paren);
863     }
864   } else {
865     safe_snprintf(current_buf, COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE, "%s/%s", type_colored, name_colored);
866   }
867 
868   return current_buf;
869 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFERS
870 #undef COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE
871 }






References colored_string(), COLORIZE_BUFFER_SIZE,
    COLORIZE_BUFFERS, LOG_COLOR_DEV, LOG_COLOR_GREY,
    LOG_COLOR_WARN, and safe_snprintf().

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Format a message using va_list.


Parameters


format Format string


args Variable arguments list


Returns


Formatted message string (must be freed by caller)


Definition at line 248 of file log/log.c.


248                                                        {
249   if (!format) {
250     return NULL;
251   }
252 
253   // First, determine the size needed
254   va_list args_copy;
255   va_copy(args_copy, args);
256   int size = safe_vsnprintf(NULL, 0, format, args_copy);
257   va_end(args_copy);
258 
259   if (size < 0) {
260     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
261     return NULL;
262   }
263 
264   // Allocate and format the message
265   char *message = SAFE_MALLOC(size + 1, char *);
266   int result = safe_vsnprintf(message, (size_t)size + 1, format, args);
267   if (result < 0) {
268     SAFE_FREE(message);
269     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format context message");
270     return NULL;
271   }
272 
273   return message;
274 }

size_t
  get_current_time_formatted (char * time_buf)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Get current time as formatted string.


Parameters


time_buf Output buffer for formatted time


Returns


Number of characters written (excluding null
  terminator)


Definition at line 216 of file log/log.c.


216                                                   {
217   /* Get wall-clock time in nanoseconds */
218   uint64_t ts_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
219   // Extract seconds and nanoseconds from total nanoseconds
220   time_t seconds = (time_t)(ts_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT);
221   long nanoseconds = (long)(ts_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
222   struct tm tm_info;
223   platform_localtime(&seconds, &tm_info);
224   // Format the time part first
225   // strftime returns 0 on error, not negative (and len is size_t/unsigned)
226   size_t len = strftime(time_buf, 32, "%H:%M:%S", &tm_info);
227   if (len == 0 || len >= 32) {
228     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format time");
229     return 0;
230   }
231 
232   // Add microseconds manually (convert nanoseconds to microseconds for display)
233   long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
234   if (microseconds < 0)
235     microseconds = 0;
236   if (microseconds > 999999)
237     microseconds = 999999;
238 
239   int result = safe_snprintf(time_buf + len, 32 - len, ".%06ld", microseconds);
240   if (result < 0 || result >= (int)(32 - len)) {
241     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format microseconds");
242     return 0;
243   }
244 
245   return len + (size_t)result;
246 }

void
  log_destroy (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Destroy the logging system and close log file.


Definition at line 623 of file log/log.c.


623                        {
624   // Destroy mmap logging first (if active)
625   if (log_mmap_is_active()) {
626     log_mmap_destroy();
627   }
628 
629   // Cleanup grep filter
630   grep_destroy();
631 
632   /* Mark logging system as shutdown FIRST (state machine transition)
633    * This signals worker threads to stop logging immediately before we free memory.
634    * This prevents TOCTOU race where a worker thread could see non-NULL format pointer
635    * after we've freed it but before lifecycle_shutdown() is called. */
636   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.lifecycle);
637 
638   // Cleanup custom format structures: All worker threads have been signaled to stop by this point.
639   // Safe to free templates now without use-after-free concerns.
640   if (g_log.format) {
641     log_template_free(g_log.format);
642     g_log.format = NULL;
643   }
644   if (g_log.format_console_only) {
645     log_template_free(g_log.format_console_only);
646     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
647   }
648   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.has_custom_format, false);
649 
650   // Lock-free cleanup using atomic operations
651   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
652   if (old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
653     platform_close(old_file);
654   }
655   atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
656 
657   // Destroy rotation mutex
658   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle);
659 }

asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap (const char *
  log_path)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap-based logging. When enabled, log messages
    bypass the mutex and use atomic operations to write directly to a
    memory-mapped log file as human-readable text.




Benefits:


    
  
  • No mutex contention between logging threads
    • 
  
  • Crash-safe: text is written directly to mmap'd file, readable after
    crash
    • 
  
  • Fast path uses atomic fetch_add, no locks
    • 
  
  • ERROR/FATAL messages sync immediately for visibility
    • 
  
  • Simple: log file IS the mmap file (no separate binary format)
    • 



Parameters


log_path Path to the log file (will be
  memory-mapped)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Note


Call log_init() first, then
  log_enable_mmap() to upgrade to lock-free


Definition at line 1758 of file log/log.c.


1758                                                         {
1759   return log_enable_mmap_sized(log_path, 0); /* Use default size */
1760 }






References log_enable_mmap_sized().






asciichat_error_t log_enable_mmap_sized (const char *
  log_path, size_t max_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Enable lock-free mmap logging with custom file size.


Parameters


log_path Path to the log file


max_size Maximum file size in bytes (0 = default 4MB)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1762 of file log/log.c.


1762                                                                                {
1763   if (!log_path) {
1764     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "log_path is required");
1765   }
1766 
1767   // Initialize mmap logging - text is written directly to the mmap'd file
1768   asciichat_error_t result = log_mmap_init_simple(log_path, max_size);
1769   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1770     return result;
1771   }
1772 
1773   log_info("Lock-free mmap logging enabled: %s", log_path);
1774   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1775 }

    log_info, log_mmap_init_simple(), and SET_ERRNO.

void

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Get the current log format template (opaque pointer).


Returns


Opaque pointer to the current log format template (may be
  NULL if not set) Cast to log_template_t* after including
  log/format.h


Returns the compiled log format template used by the logging
    system. This is useful for code that needs to format log entries using the
    same template as the rest of the logging system (e.g., platform code).




Note


The returned pointer is valid only for the lifetime of
  the logging system



It's safe to call before log_init() (will return NULL)




Return type is void* (opaque) to avoid circular dependency with
    format.h

void
  log_init (const char * filename, log_level_t level, bool
  force_stderr, bool use_mmap)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Initialize the logging system.


Parameters


filename Log file path (or NULL for no file
  logging)


level Minimum log level to output


force_stderr If true, route ALL logs to stderr (for client mode to keep
  stdout clean)


use_mmap If true, use fully lock-free mmap logging (recommended). If mmap
  fails, uses stderr only (no mutex fallback).


Note


When use_mmap=true, the entire logging path is lock-free:

    
  
  • File output uses atomic operations on mmap'd memory
    • 
  
  • Terminal output uses atomic fprintf/fwrite to fd
    • 
  
  • No mutex is ever acquired in the hot path
    • 





Definition at line 533 of file log/log.c.


533                                                                                          {
534   // Initialize rotation mutex (only operation that uses a mutex)
535   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync_type = LIFECYCLE_SYNC_MUTEX;
536   g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle.sync.mutex = &g_log.rotation_mutex;
537   lifecycle_init(&g_log.rotation_mutex_lifecycle, "log_rotation");
538 
539   // Set basic config using atomic stores
540   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.force_stderr, force_stderr);
541   bool preserve_terminal_output = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled);
542 
543   // Force logs to stderr if stdout is piped/redirected
544   // This prevents logs from contaminating piped/redirected output in snapshot/batch modes
545   // Per terminal.h: "force stderr when piped to prevent data corruption"
546   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
547     atomic_store_bool(&g_log.force_stderr, true);
548   }
549 
550   // Close any existing file (atomic load/store)
551   int old_file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
552   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle) && old_file >= 0 && old_file != STDERR_FILENO) {
553     platform_close(old_file);
554     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1);
555   }
556 
557   // Check LOG_LEVEL environment variable
558   const char *env_level_str = SAFE_GETENV("LOG_LEVEL");
559   if (env_level_str) {
560     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)parse_log_level_from_env());
561   } else {
562     atomic_store_u64(&g_log.level, (int)level);
563   }
564 
565   atomic_store_bool(&g_log.level_manually_set, false);
566   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, 0);
567 
568   if (filename) {
569     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_log.filename, filename, sizeof(g_log.filename) - 1);
570 
571     if (use_mmap) {
572       // Lock-free mmap path - writes go to mmap'd file
573       asciichat_error_t mmap_result = log_mmap_init_simple(filename, 0);
574       if (mmap_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
575         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, -1); // No regular fd - using mmap for file output
576       } else {
577         // Mmap failed - use stderr only (atomic writes, lock-free)
578         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
579           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Mmap logging failed for %s, using stderr only (lock-free)\n", filename);
580         }
581         atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
582         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
583       }
584     } else {
585       // Lock-free file I/O path - uses atomic write() syscalls
586       int fd = platform_open("log_file", filename, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
587       atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, (fd >= 0) ? fd : STDERR_FILENO);
588       if (fd < 0) {
589         if (preserve_terminal_output) {
590           safe_fprintf(stderr, "Failed to open log file: %s\n", filename);
591         }
592         g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
593       }
594     }
595   } else {
596     atomic_store_int(&g_log.file, STDERR_FILENO);
597     g_log.filename[0] = '\0';
598   }
599 
600   /* Initialize default log format (NULL means use mode-specific default) */
601   log_set_format(NULL, false);
602 
603   /* Mark logging system as initialized (state machine transition) */
604   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_log.lifecycle)) {
605     lifecycle_init(&g_log.lifecycle, "logging");
606   }
607 
608   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled, preserve_terminal_output);
609 
610   // Reset terminal detection if needed
611   if (g_log.terminal_caps_initialized && !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
612     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = false;
613   }
614 
615   // Detect terminal capabilities
616   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
617 
618   // NOTE: Color initialization happens separately via log_set_color_scheme()
619   // after options are parsed. Logging works without colors until then.
620   // NOTE: Grep filter initialization happens in main.c after options_init() completes.
621 }

1674     return;
1675   }

1678     return;
1679   }

1681   /* Get active color scheme - this ensures color system is initialized */

1683   if (!scheme) {
1684     /* Don't mark as initialized if we can't get a color scheme - return NULL instead */
1685     return;
1686   }
1687 
1688   /* Acquire mutex for compilation (mutex is now initialized by colorscheme_init) */
1689   mutex_lock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);

1702   terminal_color_mode_t mode;

1704     mode = TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR;
1705   } else if (g_log.terminal_caps.color_level >= TERM_COLOR_256) {
1706     mode = TERM_COLOR_256;
1707   } else {
1708     mode = TERM_COLOR_16;
1709   }
1710   /* Compile the color scheme to ANSI codes */
1711   asciichat_error_t result = colorscheme_compile_scheme(scheme, mode, background, &g_log.compiled_colors);
1712   g_log.log_colorscheme_initialized = true;
1713   mutex_unlock(&g_colorscheme_mutex);
1714 
1715   /* Log outside of mutex lock to avoid recursive lock deadlock */

bool log_lock_terminal (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Lock terminal output for exclusive access by the calling thread.
    Call this before interactive prompts (like password entry, yes/no questions)
    to ensure only the calling thread can output to the terminal. Other threads'
    log messages will be buffered and flushed when the terminal is unlocked.




While locked:


    
  
  • The locking thread can use log_plain() to write to terminal
    • 
  
  • Other threads' log messages go to log file and are buffered
    • 
  
  • Buffered messages are flushed to terminal on unlock
    • 



Must be paired with log_unlock_terminal().




Returns


The previous terminal lock state (for nested calls)

786   bool expected = atomic_load_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked);
787   while (!atomic_cas_bool(&g_log.terminal_locked, &expected, true)) {
788     // Retry if CAS failed
789   }
790   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread, (uint64_t)asciichat_thread_self());
791   return expected;
792 }

1090         } else {

1103   get_current_time_formatted(time_buf);

1105   char log_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1106   va_list args;

1109   if (header_len < 0 || header_len >= (int)sizeof(log_buffer)) {
1110     LOGGING_INTERNAL_ERROR(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to format log header");
1111     va_end(args);
1112     return;
1113   }
1114   int msg_len = header_len;

1116   if (formatted_len < 0) {

1119     return;

1129       log_buffer[msg_len] = '\0';

1138 
1139   // For JSON output: strip automatic trailing newline (keep explicit ones)
1140   char json_message_buf[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE];
1141   const char *json_message = user_message;

1165   // If JSON format is enabled, output ONLY JSON (skip text output)
1166   if (json_format_enabled) {
1167     // Output JSON to the JSON file descriptor
1168     log_json_write(json_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1169     // Also output JSON to console using unified routing logic, respecting quiet flag
1170     if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1171       int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(level);
1172       log_json_write(console_fd, level, time_ns, file, line, func, json_message);
1173     }
1174   } else {
1175     // Text format: output to file and terminal
1176     // No heap allocation - use log_buffer directly
1177     // Write to file (atomic write syscall) - use original buffer (with ANSI codes)
1178     int file_fd = (int)atomic_load_u64(&g_log.file);
1179     if (file_fd >= 0 && file_fd != STDERR_FILENO) {
1180       write_to_log_file_atomic(log_buffer, msg_len, NULL);
1181     }
1182 
1183     // Write to terminal (atomic state checks)
1184     va_list args_terminal;
1185     va_start(args_terminal, fmt);
1186     write_to_terminal_atomic(level, time_buf, file, line, func, fmt, args_terminal, time_ns);
1187     va_end(args_terminal);
1188   }
1189 }

int
  log_named_format_message (const char * message, char * output, size_t
  output_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format hex addresses in a message as named object
  descriptions.


Parameters


message Input message (may contain hex addresses
  like 0x123456)


output Output buffer for formatted message


output_size Size of output buffer (must be > 0)


Returns


Length of output string (excluding null terminator) if
  any formatting was applied, -1 if no formatting was applied or on error


Scans the input message for hex addresses in the format
    0x[0-9a-fA-F]+. For each address found, checks the named object registry. If
    registered, replaces the address with "type: name (0xaddress)"
    format. If not registered, leaves the original hex address unchanged.




The output buffer is always null-terminated.




Note


The formatting is idempotent - applying it twice gives
  the same result



This function is efficient for messages with few hex addresses
    (common case)




Works correctly with 32-bit and 64-bit address formats




Definition at line 467 of file log/named.c.


467                                                                                     {
468   if (!message || !output || output_size == 0) {
469     return -1;
470   }
471 
472   size_t out_pos = 0;
473   const char *p = message;
474   bool any_transformed = false;
475 
476   while (*p && out_pos < output_size - 1) {
477     /* Look for "0x" prefix indicating a hex address */
478     if (*p == '0' && *(p + 1) == 'x' && isxdigit(*(p + 2))) {
479       const char *hex_start = p;
480       if (try_format_hex_address(message, hex_start, output, output_size, &out_pos, &p)) {
481         any_transformed = true;
482         continue;
483       }
484 
485       /* Not registered or formatting failed - copy original hex address */
486       const char *hex_end = hex_start + 2; /* Start after "0x" */
487       while (*hex_end && isxdigit(*hex_end)) {
488         hex_end++;
489       }
490       size_t hex_len = hex_end - hex_start;
491       if (hex_len < output_size - out_pos) {
492         memcpy(output + out_pos, hex_start, hex_len);
493         out_pos += hex_len;
494         p = hex_end;
495       } else {
496         /* Buffer overflow - truncate and stop */
497         break;
498       }
499     } else if (isdigit(*p)) {
500       /* Look for decimal integers that might be FDs or packet types */
501       const char *int_start = p;
502       int fd_value = 0;
503       int digit_count = 0;
504 
505       /* Parse decimal digits */
506       while (*p && isdigit(*p) && digit_count < 6) { /* FDs rarely exceed 999999 */
507         fd_value = fd_value * 10 + (*p - '0');
508         p++;
509         digit_count++;
510       }
511 
512       /* Skip if this integer is part of already-formatted output like "(fd=20)" or "(type/name (...))" */
513       bool is_already_formatted = false;
514       if (int_start - message >= 5) {
515         /* Check for "(fd=", "(pkt_type=", "(socket=", "(sockfd=", or "(type/" patterns to prevent re-formatting.
516          * Named objects are formatted as: type/name (0xaddress) or type/name (key=value)
517          * We need to detect if we're already inside a formatted output.
518          */
519         const char *check = int_start - 1;
520         while (check > message && isspace(*check))
521           check--;
522 
523         // Only access *check if pointer is within bounds
524         if (check >= message && *check == '=') {
525           /* Found "=", now check what prefix it has for patterns like "(fd=20)" or "(socket=20)" or "(pkt_type=123)" */
526           const char *eq_pos = check;
527           check--;
528           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
529             check--;
530           }
531           check++;
532 
533           size_t prefix_len = eq_pos - check;
534           if ((prefix_len == 2 && strncmp(check, "fd", 2) == 0) ||
535               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "socket", 6) == 0) ||
536               (prefix_len == 6 && strncmp(check, "sockfd", 6) == 0) ||
537               (prefix_len == 8 && strncmp(check, "pkt_type", 8) == 0)) {
538             is_already_formatted = true;
539           }
540         } else if (check >= message && *check == '/') {
541           /* Found "/", now check if this is a type/name pattern.
542            * Format is "(type/name (...)" where type is word characters (thread, mutex, socket, etc.)
543            * Must verify "/" is preceded by word characters AND "(" to ensure it's a formatted type.
544            */
545           const char *slash_pos = check;
546           check--;
547           while (check > message && (isalnum(*check) || *check == '_')) {
548             check--;
549           }
550           check++;
551 
552           /* Check that the "/" was preceded by word characters (indicating a type name) */
553           size_t type_len = slash_pos - check;
554           if (type_len > 0 && (isalpha(*check) || *check == '_')) {
555             /* Verify this is a registered type/name pattern by checking for "(" before the type
556              * AND checking if the type actually exists in the registry */
557             const char *type_start = check;
558             const char *before_type = type_start - 1;
559             while (before_type > message && isspace(*before_type)) {
560               before_type--;
561             }
562 
563             /* Only mark as formatted if:
564              * 1. Preceded by "(" - ensures pattern looks like "(type/name ...)"
565              * 2. Type exists in registry - prevents false positives on random text */
566             if (before_type >= message && *before_type == '(' && is_type_in_registry(type_start, type_len)) {
567               is_already_formatted = true;
568             }
569           }
570         }
571       }
572 
573       /* Check if this is a registered packet type */
574       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_packet_type_prefix(message, int_start)) {
575         const char *name = named_get_packet_type(fd_value);
576         if (name) {
577           const char *prefix_start = find_packet_type_prefix_start(message, int_start);
578           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
579           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
580 
581           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
582             out_pos -= prefix_len;
583           }
584 
585           if (write_formatted_packet_type(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
586             any_transformed = true;
587             continue;
588           }
589 
590           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
591         }
592       }
593 
594 
595       /* Check if this is a registered file descriptor */
596       if (!is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0 && has_fd_prefix(message, int_start)) {
597         const char *name = named_get_fd(fd_value);
598         if (name) {
599           const char *prefix_start = find_fd_prefix_start(message, int_start);
600           size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
601           size_t saved_pos = out_pos;
602 
603           if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
604             out_pos -= prefix_len;
605           }
606 
607           if (write_formatted_fd(fd_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
608             any_transformed = true;
609             continue;
610           }
611 
612           out_pos = saved_pos; /* Restore on failure */
613         }
614       }
615 
616       /* Check if this integer has a generic type prefix (socket, client, connection, etc.)
617        * DISABLED: This feature has a backtracking bug that corrupts output when applied after
618        * other formatting operations. Re-enable only after fixing the backtracking logic to
619        * properly track correspondence between input and output positions.
620        * See commit b86fed2a8 which introduced the bug.
621        */
622       // NOLINTNEXTLINE(readability-simplify-boolean-expr) - Intentionally disabled code, see comment above
623       if (false && !is_already_formatted && digit_count > 0) {
624         const char *type_name = NULL;
625         size_t type_len = 0;
626         const char *prefix_start = find_generic_type_prefix(message, int_start, &type_name, &type_len);
627 
628         if (type_name && prefix_start != int_start) {
629           /* Skip if already formatted with a name (e.g., "socket/listener.0" already has a /) */
630           bool skip_format = false;
631           /* Check if this looks like it's already been formatted by looking for / in the pattern */
632           const char *check_ptr = prefix_start;
633           while (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr && *check_ptr != '/') {
634             check_ptr++;
635           }
636           if (check_ptr < int_start && *check_ptr == '/') {
637             /* Already formatted (contains /), skip */
638             skip_format = true;
639           }
640 
641           if (!skip_format) {
642             /* We found a type prefix like "socket", "client", etc.
643              * Try to look up a registered name for this type+id */
644 
645             /* Normalize type name aliases: "sockfd" → "socket", "descriptor" → "fd" */
646             const char *lookup_type = type_name;
647             size_t lookup_len = type_len;
648             if (type_len == 6 && strncmp(type_name, "sockfd", 6) == 0) {
649               lookup_type = "socket";
650               lookup_len = 6;
651             } else if (type_len == 10 && strncmp(type_name, "descriptor", 10) == 0) {
652               lookup_type = "fd";
653               lookup_len = 2;
654             }
655 
656             const char *name = named_get_by_type_and_id(lookup_type, lookup_len, fd_value);
657 
658             char temp_output[512];
659             char type_str[32];
660             char id_buffer[64];
661 
662             /* Safely copy type name */
663             if (type_len < sizeof(type_str)) {
664               memcpy(type_str, type_name, type_len);
665               type_str[type_len] = '\0';
666 
667               int id_written = snprintf(id_buffer, sizeof(id_buffer), "%d", fd_value);
668               if (id_written > 0 && id_written < (int)sizeof(id_buffer)) {
669                 int temp_written = 0;
670                 if (name) {
671                   /* Format with name: type/name (type=value) */
672                   temp_written =
673                       snprintf(temp_output, sizeof(temp_output), "%s/%s (%s=%s)", type_str, name, type_str, id_buffer);
674 
675                   if (temp_written > 0 && (size_t)temp_written < sizeof(temp_output)) {
676                     /* Backtrack to remove the prefix we already copied */
677                     size_t prefix_len = int_start - prefix_start;
678                     if (out_pos >= prefix_len) {
679                       out_pos -= prefix_len;
680                     }
681 
682                     int copy_len = temp_written;
683                     if (out_pos + copy_len < output_size - 1) {
684                       memcpy(output + out_pos, temp_output, copy_len);
685                       out_pos += copy_len;
686                       any_transformed = true;
687                       continue;
688                     }
689                   }
690                 }
691               }
692             }
693           }
694         }
695       }
696 
697       /* Not a registered FD or formatting failed - copy original number */
698       if (!copy_unformatted_decimal(int_start, p, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
699         break;
700       }
701     } else {
702       /* Check if this is the start of "type=DIGIT" pattern */
703       if (*p == 't' && p + 4 < message + strlen(message) && is_type_equals_pattern(p)) {
704         if (!type_equals_inside_parens(p, message)) {
705           const char *digit_end = p + 5;
706           int pkt_value = parse_decimal_digits(digit_end, &digit_end);
707 
708           if (pkt_value > 0) {
709             const char *name = named_get_packet_type(pkt_value);
710             if (name && write_formatted_type_equals(pkt_value, name, output, output_size, &out_pos)) {
711               p = digit_end;
712               any_transformed = true;
713               continue;
714             }
715           }
716         }
717       }
718 
719       /* Regular character - copy it */
720       copy_char_to_output(output, &out_pos, *p++);
721     }
722   }
723 
724   /* Null terminate */
725   if (out_pos < output_size) {
726     output[out_pos] = '\0';
727   } else {
728     output[output_size - 1] = '\0';
729   }
730 
731   return any_transformed ? (int)out_pos : -1;
732 }

const
  char * log_named_format_or_original (const char * message)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/named.h>


Format named objects in a message (thread-local buffer).


Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string if any formatting was applied,
  original message if no formatting was applied


Convenience wrapper that handles buffer management internally
    using a thread-local buffer. The returned pointer is valid only until the
    next call to this function from the same thread.




Safe to use directly in log macros:




log_info("State: %s", log_named_format_or_original(msg));



Note


Maximum formatted message length is 4096 bytes



If format buffer overflows, returns original message




Parameters


message Input message


Returns


Formatted message string (thread-local buffer, valid only
  until next call)


Convenience wrapper that uses a thread-local buffer internally.
    Applies formatting iteratively until the message stops changing (fixpoint).
    Returns the original message if no formatting was applied.


Definition at line 743 of file log/named.c.


743                                                               {
744   if (!message) {
745     return message;
746   }
747 
748   static _Thread_local char format_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
749   static _Thread_local char work_buffer[NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE];
750 
751   /* Apply formatting iteratively until the message stabilizes (no more changes) */
752   const char *current = message;
753   size_t max_iterations = 3; /* Reduce iterations to prevent recursion (prevent transforming already-transformed output) */
754 
755   for (size_t iter = 0; iter < max_iterations; iter++) {
756     int result = log_named_format_message(current, format_buffer, sizeof(format_buffer));
757 
758     if (result <= 0) {
759       /* No transformation was made, we've reached a fixpoint */
760       return current == message ? message : format_buffer;
761     }
762 
763     /* Check if the message changed */
764     if (strcmp(current, format_buffer) == 0) {
765       /* No actual change, return the current version */
766       return format_buffer;
767     }
768 
769     /* Message changed, prepare for next iteration */
770     if (current == message) {
771       /* First iteration, use format_buffer as input for next iteration */
772       current = format_buffer;
773     } else {
774       /* Subsequent iterations, swap buffers */
775       strcpy(work_buffer, format_buffer);
776       current = work_buffer;
777     }
778   }
779 
780   /* Max iterations reached, return last formatted version */
781   return format_buffer;
782 }

    NAMED_FORMAT_BUFFER_SIZE.

1255       }

1257       write_to_log_file_atomic("\n", 1, NULL);
1258     }
1259   }
1260 
1261   // Terminal output (atomic state checks)
1262   if (!atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_output_enabled)) {
1263     return;
1264   }
1265   if (atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_locked)) {
1266     uint64_t owner = atomic_load_u64(&g_log.terminal_owner_thread);

1278     uint64_t time_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1279     int console_fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1280     log_json_write(console_fd, LOG_INFO, time_ns, __FILE__, __LINE__, "log_plain_msg", log_buffer);
1281   } else {
1282     // Choose output stream using unified routing logic (LOG_INFO level)
1283     int fd = terminal_choose_log_fd(LOG_INFO);
1284     FILE *output_stream = (fd == STDERR_FILENO) ? stderr : stdout;
1285 
1286     // Apply colorization for TTY output
1287     if (terminal_should_color_output(fd)) {
1288       const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(log_buffer);
1289       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", colorized_msg);
1290     } else {
1291       safe_fprintf(output_stream, "%s\n", log_buffer);
1292     }
1293     (void)fflush(output_stream);
1294   }
1295 }

size_t
  log_recolor_plain_entry (const char * plain_line, char * colored_buf, size_t
  buf_size)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Recolor a plain (non-colored) log line with proper ANSI codes.
    Converts a plain text log line (from log file) into a colored version
    matching the format used for terminal output. Applies colors to:


    
  
  • Timestamp and level based on log level
    • 
  
  • Thread ID in grey
    • 
  
  • File path in cyan
    • 
  
  • Line number in magenta
    • 
  
  • Function name in orange/DEV color
    • 
  
  • Message body colorized appropriately
    • 



Expected plain format (debug mode): [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL]
    [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE




Parameters


plain_line Plain text log line (from log file)


colored_buf Output buffer for colored version (must be large enough)


buf_size Size of colored_buf


Returns


Length of colored string, 0 on error or invalid
  format


Note


Uses static buffer internally, result is reused across
  calls


Parses plain log format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID]
    FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE And recolors it with appropriate ANSI codes
    matching the colored format.


Definition at line 1864 of file log/log.c.


1864                                                                                            {
1865   if (!plain_line || !colored_buf || buf_size < 128) {
1866     return 0;
1867   }
1868 
1869   static char work_buffer[LOG_MSG_BUFFER_SIZE + 1024];
1870 
1871   // Parse format FIRST, regardless of color availability
1872   // Parse format: [TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [tid:THREAD_ID] FILE:LINE in FUNC(): MESSAGE
1873   const char *p = plain_line;
1874 
1875   // Extract timestamp [TIMESTAMP]
1876   if (*p != '[') {
1877     return 0; // Invalid format
1878   }
1879   p++;
1880   const char *timestamp_start = p;
1881 
1882   // Find the closing ] for timestamp - look for pattern that ends with proper timestamp format
1883   // Valid timestamp: HH:MM:SS.UUUUUU (time with microseconds, no date)
1884   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1885     p++;
1886   }
1887   if (*p != ']') {
1888     return 0; // Malformed - no closing bracket
1889   }
1890 
1891   size_t timestamp_len = p - timestamp_start;
1892   char timestamp[64];
1893   if (timestamp_len >= sizeof(timestamp) || timestamp_len == 0) {
1894     return 0; // Invalid timestamp - empty or too long
1895   }
1896   SAFE_STRNCPY(timestamp, timestamp_start, timestamp_len);
1897   timestamp[timestamp_len] = '\0';
1898   p++; // Skip ]
1899 
1900   // Skip whitespace after timestamp
1901   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1902     p++;
1903   }
1904 
1905   // Extract level [LEVEL]
1906   if (*p != '[') {
1907     return 0; // Missing opening bracket for level
1908   }
1909   p++;
1910   const char *level_start = p;
1911   while (*p && *p != ']') {
1912     p++;
1913   }
1914   if (*p != ']') {
1915     return 0; // Missing closing bracket for level
1916   }
1917   size_t level_len = p - level_start;
1918   char level_str[16];
1919   if (level_len >= sizeof(level_str) || level_len == 0) {
1920     return 0; // Level string too long or empty
1921   }
1922   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1923   strncpy(level_str, level_start, level_len);
1924   level_str[level_len] = '\0';
1925 
1926   // Determine log level for color selection
1927   log_level_t level = LOG_INFO; // Default
1928   if (strstr(level_str, "DEV") || strstr(level_str, "DEBUG")) {
1929     level = LOG_DEBUG;
1930   } else if (strstr(level_str, "INFO")) {
1931     level = LOG_INFO;
1932   } else if (strstr(level_str, "WARN")) {
1933     level = LOG_WARN;
1934   } else if (strstr(level_str, "ERROR")) {
1935     level = LOG_ERROR;
1936   } else if (strstr(level_str, "FATAL")) {
1937     level = LOG_FATAL;
1938   }
1939 
1940   p++; // Skip ]
1941 
1942   // Skip whitespace after level
1943   while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1944     p++;
1945   }
1946 
1947   // Extract thread ID [tid:THREAD_ID] - optional field
1948   uint64_t tid = 0;
1949   if (*p == '[' && strncmp(p, "[tid:", 5) == 0) {
1950     p += 5;
1951     char *tid_end = NULL;
1952     tid = strtoull(p, &tid_end, 10);
1953     if (!tid_end || *tid_end != ']') {
1954       // tid parsing failed, but continue anyway (might still recover)
1955       // Try to find the closing bracket
1956       while (*p && *p != ']') {
1957         p++;
1958       }
1959       if (*p == ']') {
1960         p++;
1961       }
1962     } else {
1963       p = tid_end + 1; // Skip past the ]
1964     }
1965     // Skip whitespace after tid
1966     while (*p && *p == ' ') {
1967       p++;
1968     }
1969   }
1970   // tid is optional - continue parsing if not present
1971 
1972   // Extract file path (everything up to :LINE)
1973   const char *file_start = p;
1974   while (*p && *p != ':') {
1975     p++;
1976   }
1977   if (*p != ':') {
1978     return 0; // Malformed
1979   }
1980   size_t file_len = p - file_start;
1981   char file_path[256];
1982   if (file_len >= sizeof(file_path)) {
1983     return 0;
1984   }
1985   /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
1986   strncpy(file_path, file_start, file_len);
1987   file_path[file_len] = '\0';
1988   p++; // Skip :
1989 
1990   // Extract line number (digits only)
1991   int line_num = 0;
1992   const char *line_start = p;
1993   while (*p && isdigit((unsigned char)*p)) {
1994     p++;
1995   }
1996   if (p == line_start) {
1997     return 0; // No line number
1998   }
1999   line_num = (int)strtol(line_start, NULL, 10);
2000 
2001   // Skip whitespace and find "in" keyword (be very lenient)
2002   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t' || *p == '\n' || *p == '\r')) {
2003     p++;
2004   }
2005 
2006   // Try to find "in " - if not found, might still be valid, just harder to parse
2007   if (strncmp(p, "in ", 3) == 0) {
2008     p += 3; // Skip "in "
2009   } else if (*p == 'i' && *(p + 1) == 'n' && (*(p + 2) == ' ' || *(p + 2) == '\t')) {
2010     // Allow tab after "in"
2011     p += 2; // Skip "in"
2012     while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == '\t')) {
2013       p++;
2014     }
2015   } else {
2016     // Missing "in" keyword - this is a format error
2017     return 0;
2018   }
2019 
2020   // Extract function name (everything up to "()")
2021   const char *func_start = p;
2022   while (*p && *p != '(') {
2023     p++;
2024   }
2025   if (*p != '(' || func_start == p) {
2026     return 0; // Missing function name or parentheses
2027   }
2028   size_t func_len = p - func_start;
2029 
2030   // Trim trailing whitespace from function name
2031   while (func_len > 0 && (func_start[func_len - 1] == ' ' || func_start[func_len - 1] == '\t')) {
2032     func_len--;
2033   }
2034 
2035   char func_name[256];
2036   if (func_len >= sizeof(func_name)) {
2037     func_len = sizeof(func_name) - 1;
2038   }
2039   if (func_len > 0) {
2040     /* Use strncpy directly for substring extraction (not null-terminated source) */
2041     strncpy(func_name, func_start, func_len);
2042   }
2043   func_name[func_len] = '\0';
2044 
2045   // Skip "(" and ")" - be lenient about what's between them
2046   if (*p == '(') {
2047     p++;
2048     while (*p && *p != ')') {
2049       p++;
2050     }
2051     if (*p == ')') {
2052       p++;
2053     }
2054   }
2055 
2056   // Skip whitespace and optional colon(s) and other separators
2057   while (*p && (*p == ' ' || *p == ':' || *p == '\t')) {
2058     p++;
2059   }
2060 
2061   // Remaining is the message
2062   const char *message = p;
2063 
2064   // Format is valid, get colors from logging system
2065   const char **colors = log_get_color_array();
2066   if (!colors) {
2067     // No colors available, return plain text
2068     static bool warned_once = false;
2069     if (!warned_once) {
2070       log_debug("WARNING: log_recolor_plain_entry() called but colors not initialized - returning plain text");
2071       warned_once = true;
2072     }
2073     size_t len = strlen(plain_line);
2074     if (len >= buf_size) {
2075       return 0;
2076     }
2077     SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, plain_line, buf_size - 1);
2078     colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2079     return len;
2080   }
2081 
2082   // Build colored output
2083   const char *level_color = colors[level];
2084   const char *reset = colors[LOG_COLOR_RESET];
2085   const char *file_color = colors[1]; // DEBUG/Cyan
2086   const char *line_color = colors[6]; // GREY (matching tid)
2087   const char *func_color = colors[0]; // DEV/Orange
2088   const char *tid_color = colors[6];  // GREY
2089 
2090   int len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2091                           "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp,
2092                           reset, level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color,
2093                           file_path, reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, message);
2094 
2095   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer)) {
2096     return 0;
2097   }
2098 
2099   // Colorize the message part
2100   const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(message);
2101   len = safe_snprintf(work_buffer, sizeof(work_buffer),
2102                       "[%s%s%s] [%s%s%s] [tid:%s%llu%s] %s%s%s:%s%d%s in %s%s%s(): %s", level_color, timestamp, reset,
2103                       level_color, level_str, reset, tid_color, (unsigned long long)tid, reset, file_color, file_path,
2104                       reset, line_color, line_num, reset, func_color, func_name, reset, colorized_msg);
2105 
2106   if (len <= 0 || len >= (int)sizeof(work_buffer) || len >= (int)buf_size) {
2107     return 0;
2108   }
2109 
2110   SAFE_STRNCPY(colored_buf, work_buffer, buf_size - 1);
2111   colored_buf[buf_size - 1] = '\0';
2112   return (size_t)len;
2113 }

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Re-detect terminal capabilities after logging is initialized.

1599                                               {
1600   // Guard against recursion
1601   if (g_log.terminal_caps_detecting) {
1602     return;
1603   }
1604 
1605   // Detect if not initialized, or if we're using defaults (not reliably detected)
1606   // This ensures we get proper detection after logging is ready, replacing any defaults
1607   // Once we have reliable detection, never re-detect to keep colors consistent
1608   if (!g_log.terminal_caps_initialized || !g_log.terminal_caps.detection_reliable) {
1609     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = true;
1610     g_log.terminal_caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1611     g_log.terminal_caps_detecting = false;
1612     g_log.terminal_caps_initialized = true;
1613 
1614     // Now log the capabilities AFTER colors are set, so this log uses the correct colors
1615     log_debug("Terminal capabilities: color_level=%d, capabilities=0x%x, utf8=%s, fps=%d",
1616               g_log.terminal_caps.color_level, g_log.terminal_caps.capabilities,
1617               g_log.terminal_caps.utf8_support ? "yes" : "no", g_log.terminal_caps.desired_fps);
1618 
1619     // Now that we've detected once with reliable results, keep these colors consistent for all future logs

Force all terminal log output to stderr.

751   }
752

755   bool is_custom = (format_str && format_str[0] != '\0');
756 
757   /* Parse the format string (always parse, never skip) */
758   log_template_t *parsed_format = log_template_parse(format_to_use, false);
759 
760   if (!parsed_format) {
761     log_error("Failed to parse log format: %s", format_to_use);
762     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid log format string");
763   }
764 
765   /* If console_only is true, we also need the default format for file output */
766   if (console_only && is_custom) {

771       log_error("Failed to parse default log format");
772       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to parse default format");
773     }
774     g_log.format = default_format;
775     g_log.format_console_only = parsed_format;
776   } else {
777     g_log.format = parsed_format;
778     g_log.format_console_only = NULL;
779   }
780 
781   atomic_store_u64(&g_log.has_custom_format, is_custom);
782   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
783 }

698                                            {
699   // Respect --quiet flag: if quiet is set, never enable terminal output
700   // But allow disabling even if options are unavailable (for shutdown cleanup)
701 
702   // Only check quiet flag when ENABLING output to avoid deadlock with
703   // concurrent logging from other threads (options_get() can deadlock when
704   // called while other threads are actively logging due to RCU synchronization)
705   if (enabled) {

#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Begin shutdown phase - disable console logging but keep file
    logging. Call this before logging shutdown messages. Disables console output

    interfere with output.

  buf_size, log_level_t level, const char * timestamp, const char * file,
  int line, const char * func, uint64_t tid, const char * message,
  bool use_colors, uint64_t time_nanoseconds)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/format.h>


Apply format to a log entry and write result to buffer.


Parameters


format Compiled format (from log_template_parse)


buf Output buffer


buf_size Output buffer size


level Log level


timestamp Pre-formatted timestamp string


file Source file name (or NULL)


line Source line number (or 0)


func Function name (or NULL)


tid Thread ID


message Log message text


use_colors If true, apply ANSI color codes


time_nanoseconds Raw wall-clock time in nanoseconds (for microseconds and
  nanoseconds)


Returns


Number of characters written (excluding null terminator),
  or -1 on error


Renders the compiled format using provided log entry values.
    Evaluates each specifier and writes results to output buffer.




Example:




log_template_t *fmt = log_template_parse("[%time(%H:%M:%S)] [%level] %microseconds %message", false);
char output[512];
uint64_t now = time_get_realtime_ns();
int len = log_template_apply(fmt, output, sizeof(output),


                           LOG_INFO, "14:30:45", "test.c", 42, "main",


                           1234, "Test message", false, now);



Definition at line 484 of file log/format.c.


486                                                                                         {
487   /* Check all critical pointers - format could be freed by another thread (TOCTOU race) */
488   if (!format || !buf || buf_size == 0) {
489     return -1;
490   }
491 
492   int total_written = 0;
493   char *p = buf;
494   size_t remaining = buf_size - 1; /* Reserve space for null terminator */
495 
496   (void)timestamp; /* May be used in future; for now, LOG_FORMAT_TIME uses custom formatting */
497 
498   /* Safety check: verify format->specs is valid before dereferencing
499    * This protects against use-after-free if format is freed by another thread (TOCTOU race).
500    * Between our initial NULL check and here, the main thread may have freed this memory. */
501   if (!format || !format->specs || format->spec_count == 0) {
502     return -1;
503   }
504 
505   for (size_t i = 0; i < format->spec_count; i++) {
506     /* Re-check specs pointer in loop to catch use-after-free during iteration */
507     if (!format->specs || i >= format->spec_count) {
508       break; /* Format was freed, stop processing */
509     }
510     const log_format_spec_t *spec = &format->specs[i];
511 
512     /* Additional safety check: if spec is null or invalid, stop processing */
513     if (!spec) {
514       break;
515     }
516 
517     int written = 0;
518 
519     switch (spec->type) {
520     case LOG_FORMAT_LITERAL:
521       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", spec->literal ? spec->literal : "");
522       break;
523 
524     case LOG_FORMAT_TIME:
525       /* Format time using custom time formatter */
526       if (spec->literal) {
527         written = time_format_now(spec->literal, p, remaining + 1);
528         if (written <= 0) {
529           log_debug("time_format_now failed for format: %s", spec->literal);
530           written = 0;
531         }
532       }
533       break;
534 
535     case LOG_FORMAT_LEVEL:
536       /* Log level as string (DEV, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL) */
537       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", get_level_string(level));
538       break;
539 
540     case LOG_FORMAT_LEVEL_ALIGNED:
541       /* Log level padded to exactly 5 characters (prevents truncation to [DEBU]/[ERRO]) */
542       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", get_level_string_padded(level));
543       break;
544 
545     case LOG_FORMAT_FILE:
546       if (file) {
547         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", file);
548       }
549       break;
550 
551     case LOG_FORMAT_FILE_RELATIVE:
552       if (file) {
553         const char *rel_file = extract_project_relative_path(file);
554         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", rel_file);
555       }
556       break;
557 
558     case LOG_FORMAT_LINE:
559       if (line > 0) {
560         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%d", line);
561       }
562       break;
563 
564     case LOG_FORMAT_FUNC:
565       if (func) {
566         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", func);
567       }
568       break;
569 
570     case LOG_FORMAT_TID:
571       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%llu", (unsigned long long)tid);
572       break;
573 
574     case LOG_FORMAT_TNAME: {
575       asciichat_thread_t thread = (asciichat_thread_t)tid;
576       uintptr_t key = asciichat_thread_to_key(thread);
577       const char *tname = named_get(key);
578       if (tname) {
579         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", tname);
580       } else {
581         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%llu", (unsigned long long)tid);
582       }
583       break;
584     }
585 
586     case LOG_FORMAT_MICROSECONDS: {
587       /* Extract microseconds from nanoseconds (ns_value % 1_000_000_000 / 1000) */
588       long nanoseconds = (long)(time_nanoseconds % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
589       long microseconds = nanoseconds / 1000;
590       if (microseconds < 0)
591         microseconds = 0;
592       if (microseconds > 999999)
593         microseconds = 999999;
594       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%06ld", microseconds);
595       break;
596     }
597 
598     case LOG_FORMAT_NANOSECONDS: {
599       /* Extract nanoseconds component (ns_value % 1_000_000_000) */
600       long nanoseconds = (long)(time_nanoseconds % NS_PER_SEC_INT);
601       if (nanoseconds < 0)
602         nanoseconds = 0;
603       if (nanoseconds > 999999999)
604         nanoseconds = 999999999;
605       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%09ld", nanoseconds);
606       break;
607     }
608 
609     case LOG_FORMAT_STRFTIME_CODE: {
610       /* strftime format code (like %H, %M, %S, %A, %B, etc.)
611        * Let strftime handle all the parsing and validation */
612       if (spec->literal && spec->literal_len < 64) {
613         /* Construct format string with % prefix on stack */
614         char format_str[66]; /* 1 (%) + 64 (literal) + 1 (null) */
615         format_str[0] = '%';
616         memcpy(format_str + 1, spec->literal, spec->literal_len);
617         format_str[spec->literal_len + 1] = '\0';
618         written = time_format_now(format_str, p, remaining + 1);
619         if (written <= 0) {
620           log_debug("time_format_now failed for format code: %s", format_str);
621           written = 0;
622         }
623       }
624       break;
625     }
626 
627     case LOG_FORMAT_MESSAGE:
628       if (message) {
629         /* Format hex addresses as named object descriptions (type/name format) */
630         const char *formatted_msg = log_named_format_or_original(message);
631         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", formatted_msg);
632       }
633       break;
634 
635     case LOG_FORMAT_COLORED_MESSAGE: {
636       /* Apply colorize_log_message() for number/unit/hex highlighting, then format named objects */
637       if (message) {
638         const char *formatted_msg = log_named_format_or_original(message);
639         const char *colorized_msg = colorize_log_message(formatted_msg);
640         written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", colorized_msg);
641       }
642       break;
643     }
644 
645     case LOG_FORMAT_COLORLOG_LEVEL:
646       /* Color code for the log level (placeholder for future color support) */
647       (void)use_colors; /* Suppress unused parameter warning */
648       written = 0;
649       break;
650 
651     case LOG_FORMAT_COLOR: {
652       /* Parse and apply %color(LEVEL, content)
653        * literal stores "LEVEL,content" where LEVEL is the color level name (or "*" for current level)
654        * and content is a format string to render and colorize */
655       if (!spec->literal || spec->literal_len == 0) {
656         written = 0;
657         break;
658       }
659 
660       /* Find the comma separating LEVEL and content */
661       const char *comma_pos = strchr(spec->literal, ',');
662       if (!comma_pos) {
663         /* Invalid format - no comma found */
664         log_debug("log_template_apply: %%color format missing comma in: %s", spec->literal);
665         written = 0;
666         break;
667       }
668 
669       /* Extract level name (before comma) */
670       size_t level_len = comma_pos - spec->literal;
671       char level_name[32];
672       if (level_len >= sizeof(level_name)) {
673         /* Level name too long */
674         written = 0;
675         break;
676       }
677       memcpy(level_name, spec->literal, level_len);
678       level_name[level_len] = '\0';
679 
680       /* Parse level name to log_color_t (pass current level for "*" support) */
681       log_color_t color = parse_color_level(level_name, level);
682 
683       /* Extract content (after comma), skip leading whitespace */
684       const char *content_start = comma_pos + 1;
685       while (*content_start == ' ' || *content_start == '\t') {
686         content_start++;
687       }
688 
689       /* Render content recursively */
690       char content_buf[512];
691       int content_len = render_format_content(content_start, content_buf, sizeof(content_buf), level, timestamp, file,
692                                               line, func, tid, message, use_colors, time_nanoseconds);
693 
694       if (content_len < 0 || content_len >= (int)sizeof(content_buf)) {
695         written = 0;
696         break;
697       }
698 
699       /* Apply color to rendered content using colored_string */
700       const char *colored_content = colored_string(color, content_buf);
701 
702       /* Copy colored content to output buffer */
703       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "%s", colored_content);
704       break;
705     }
706 
707     case LOG_FORMAT_NEWLINE:
708       /* Platform-aware newline */
709 #ifdef _WIN32
710       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "\r\n");
711 #else
712       written = safe_snprintf(p, remaining + 1, "\n");
713 #endif
714       break;
715 
716     default:
717       break;
718     }
719 
720     if (written < 0) {
721       /* snprintf error */
722       return -1;
723     }
724 
725     if ((size_t)written > remaining) {
726       /* Buffer overflow prevention */
727       log_debug("log_template_apply: buffer overflow prevented");
728       return -1;
729     }
730 
731     p += written;
732     remaining -= written;
733     total_written += written;
734   }
735 
736   *p = '\0';
737   return total_written;
738 }

log_template_t * log_template_parse (const char *
  format_str, bool console_only)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/format.h>


Parse a format string into compiled format structure.


Parameters


format_str Format string (e.g.,
  "[%time(%H:%M:%S)] [%level_aligned] %message")


console_only If true, format applies only to console (not file)


Returns


Compiled format, or NULL on parse error


Parses format string and compiles it into efficient specifier
    array. Format string must be valid UTF-8. Returns NULL on:


    
  
  • Invalid UTF-8 in format string
    • 
  
  • Invalid format specifiers
    • 
  
  • Malformed time(format) syntax
    • 
  
  • Memory allocation failure
    • 



Note


Errors are logged with details about what failed

void
  log_terminal_msg (log_level_t level, const char * file, int line, const
  char * func, const char * fmt, ...)


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Log a message to terminal only (no file output).


Parameters


level Log level (LOG_DEV, LOG_DEBUG, LOG_INFO,
  LOG_WARN, LOG_ERROR, LOG_FATAL)


file Source file name (or NULL to omit)


line Source line number (or 0 to omit)


func Function name (or NULL to omit)


fmt Format string (printf-style)


... Format arguments


Logs to terminal only, skipping file/mmap output. WARN/ERROR/FATAL
    go to stderr, other levels go to stdout (unless force_stderr is
  enabled).

void
  log_truncate_if_large (void )


#include <include/ascii-chat/log/log.h>


Manually truncate large log files. Checks if the log file exceeds
    MAX_LOG_SIZE and truncates it if necessary.


Definition at line 805 of file log/log.c.


805                                  {
806   // Log rotation is inherently racy without locks - best-effort only
807   // For reliable rotation, use mmap mode or external logrotate
808   int file = atomic_load_int(&g_log.file);
809   if (file >= 0 && file != STDERR_FILENO && strlen(g_log.filename) > 0) {
810     struct stat st;
811     if (fstat(file, &st) == 0 && st.st_size > MAX_LOG_SIZE) {
812       atomic_store_u64(&g_log.current_size, (size_t)st.st_size);
813     }
814   }
815 }
